title: calceolaria riparia var. houstonii
intro: Calceolaria riparia var. houstonii, also known as slipper flower, is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calceolaria riparia var. houstonii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it is often used as a tea or tincture in traditional medicine.
